findings.text: High-risk requests at or above the policy limit are now allowed. The previous behavior denied risk_score > max_risk, but this change flips the boundary to allow risk_score >= max_risk, creating an authorization bypass for the riskiest changes.
Blocked states are no longer denied. Because the blocked_states check was removed, a request in a blocked state can now be allowed when owner_approved is true or owner approval is not required.
Admin users are now denied unconditionally after the risk check. Previously admins could authorize otherwise valid changes; this reverses admin authorization semantics.
summarize_recent now returns limit + 1 items when enough results exist. The loop upper bound changed from min(limit, len(results)) to min(limit + 1, len(results)), violating the limit contract.

The new test only covers owner-approved allow behavior and misses the changed denial paths: high-risk requests, admin handling, blocked states, summarize_recent limit behavior, and audit label compatibility.
